spec_match.rationale: Concept exercises the relevant API surface (window.) with runtime script (~4616 chars) wired to live controls, in line with the feature summary.

interactive.rationale: Some controls present but the interactive surface is thin (1 buttons / 0 inputs / 1 selects). Hard to call it hands-on.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (chrome, correct, drag, dropeffect, each, left, over, right, spec, values, zone).

edge_cases.rationale: No visible edge case / fallback / error handling — only the happy path.

references.rationale: Page does not include the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link — violates the rule #3 self-heal invariant.

design_system.rationale: All colours come via 85 CSS-variable references, no raw hex, no button background override without a colour override, and not a static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface beyond one or two buttons
openQuestions.detail: Add controls that vary the parameters the spec exposes — toggles, ranges, selects — so the user can drive the demo through several distinct states instead of a single one-shot trigger.

openQuestions.title: Add explicit edge case coverage
openQuestions.detail: Pick one failure mode the spec or chromestatus motivation calls out (unsupported browser, denied permission, empty input, etc.) and surface it as a distinct demo state alongside the happy path.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link
openQuestions.detail: Page is missing the chromestatus.com/feature/6325617459068928 link inside a `references` section. CLAUDE.md rule #3 requires this on every concept so the slug-recovery script can re-derive the canonical name.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("The drag and drop specifications require the "dataTransfer"'s object "dropEffect" attribute to have certain predetermined values on "dragEnter", "dragOver" and "dragLeave". Drag enter and drag over should have a "dropEffect" based on the cu") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/dnd.html#dndevents to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~4616 chars of script across 1 button(s) and 1 other control(s). hits 3 pass / 1 partial / 2 fail across the six rubric criteria.
